operating_state: Goldman Sachs generated net revenues of $53.51 billion for 2024, 16% higher than 2023, driven by significantly higher investment banking revenues from leveraged finance and equity underwriting plus increased FICC and Equities revenues. Global Banking & Markets and Asset & Wealth Management delivered strong performance from higher management fees and equity investment gains. The firm completed strategic exits from consumer activities including GreenSky sale and GM credit card program transition while maintaining stable trading performance with daily VaR-based net revenues exceeding $100 million on 62 business days versus 52 in 2023.
cost_structure: Operating expenses decreased 2% to $33.77 billion, improving efficiency ratio to 63.1% from 74.6% in 2023, primarily due to elimination of prior year charges including GreenSky write-downs and Consumer platforms goodwill impairment. Higher compensation expenses reflected improved operating performance and transaction-based activity. The firm maintains significant ongoing investments in technology infrastructure including AI capabilities and electronic trading systems while regulatory compliance costs continue escalating across jurisdictions.
material_events: Goldman returned $11.80 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends while completing the sale of GreenSky and substantially all Marcus loan portfolio. The Federal Reserve increased the firm's stress capital buffer from 5.5% to 6.2% effective October 2024, and the G-SIB surcharge is expected to increase from 3.0% to 3.5% beginning 2026. The firm formed Capital Solutions Group in January 2025 integrating financing and origination capabilities and established new AI Risk and Controls Committee governance structures.
macro_exposures: The firm faces heightened exposure to U.S.-China trade tensions, ongoing geopolitical conflicts, and central bank policy uncertainty affecting client activity levels. Climate-related risks encompass both physical risks from climate changes affecting asset values and transition risks from decarbonization policies. Interest rate sensitivity shows asymmetric exposure with earnings-at-risk of negative $270 million for 100 basis point rate decline versus positive $140 million for increases.
forward_outlook: Management targets through-the-cycle ROE of 14-16% and ROTE of 15-17%, having achieved 12.7% ROE and 13.5% ROTE in 2024, with 2025 effective tax rate expected at approximately 21%. The firm targets growing total credit alternative assets to $300 billion by end of 2028 and expects to sell down the vast majority of historical principal investments portfolio by end of 2026. Platform Solutions targets pre-tax breakeven by end of 2025.
uncertainty_profile: Credit rating downgrades could trigger additional collateral requirements of $315 million for one-notch and $1.20 billion for two-notch downgrades as of December 2024. Basel III implementation varies across jurisdictions with EU rules effective January 2025 and US regulators expected to work on revised proposals in 2025. Resolution strategy execution under single point of entry could result in complete losses for Group Inc. security holders, with unrecognized tax benefits liability of $2.16 billion.
